name: Law Office of David Kennedy Bifulco, P.C.
description_short: Pennsylvania debtor-creditor rights attorney offering foreclosure defense, debt settlement, and FDCPA/TCPA litigation in Eastern PA counties.
description_long:
The Law Office of David Kennedy Bifulco, P.C. is a solo practice located in downtown Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, serving clients across Eastern Pennsylvania counties including Lehigh, Northampton, Bucks, Montgomery, and Philadelphia. The firm focuses on consumer debt issues and creditor-debtor legal matters, with David Kennedy Bifulco holding a five-star Avvo rating. The practice operates 24/7 for consultations and positions itself as a compassionate alternative to high-volume debt relief firms.

Bifulco's services span three primary areas: debt settlement negotiation for credit card balances, foreclosure defense to help clients retain their homes, and credit collections representation for businesses and individuals owed money. The website emphasizes personalized case handling rather than one-size-fits-all approaches, acknowledging that different debtors require different solutions. The firm also handles FDCPA (Fair Debt Collection Practices Act) and TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) cases, though these are not heavily detailed on the website.

The firm distinguishes itself through direct attorney availability, client testimonials highlighting responsiveness (same-day callbacks, weekend availability), and emphasis on being "heard and helped" rather than receiving false hope. Bifulco advertises reasonable costs relative to service quality, accepts major credit cards, and offers free initial consultations. The marketing language focuses on ethical professional conduct and emotional support during financial hardship.

A key limitation is that while the website mentions bankruptcy attorney services in the category data, the site itself contains no explicit information about Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing services—only debt settlement, foreclosure, and collections work. This suggests the firm may handle bankruptcy matters but does not prominently feature them in its online marketing. The practice appears to be a solo operation without information about associate attorneys or staffing depth, which may limit availability for complex cases.
pros: Five-star Avvo rating explicitly mentioned for professional and ethical legal services
24/7 free consultation availability with same-day or weekend callback responsiveness per client reviews
Personalized case approach—explicitly states different debtors get different solutions rather than template handling
Local presence in Bethlehem with convenient downtown location serving 5+ Eastern PA counties
Direct attorney-client contact (David Kennedy Bifulco handles calls personally per testimonials)
Reasonable fee structure emphasized in marketing copy relative to larger debt relief companies
Handles both debtor and creditor sides (debt settlement, foreclosure defense, collections work)
cons: Website does not explicitly detail bankruptcy filing services despite category assignment—unclear if Chapter 7/13 is actually offered
Solo practice with no visible associate attorney information may create availability or capacity constraints
Limited detail on actual settlement outcomes, foreclosure success rates, or case statistics
Testimonials are brief and generic; no case examples with specific dollar amounts or results
No information about attorney credentials beyond Avvo rating (bar admission date, years experience, bankruptcy-specific training)
best_for: Eastern Pennsylvania residents with credit card debt, foreclosure risk, or collections disputes seeking personalized attorney representation
Debtors who value direct attorney contact and want to avoid high-volume debt settlement mills
Individuals in Lehigh, Northampton, Bucks, or Montgomery counties needing local Pennsylvania debtor-creditor counsel
diagnosis: Best for Eastern Pennsylvania residents facing foreclosure, credit card debt, or collections suits who prefer personalized attorney handling over debt settlement company intermediation. Key caveat: the website does not clearly confirm bankruptcy filing services (Chapter 7/13) despite category placement, so verify bankruptcy representation availability before engagement if that is your primary need.
services: Debt settlement negotiation for credit card balances
Foreclosure defense and home retention assistance
FDCPA violation litigation (Fair Debt Collection Practices Act)
TCPA violation litigation (Telephone Consumer Protection Act)
